title: SPARQL temporal functions break on non-temporal args
description:
The following query fails with Virtuoso 22007 Error DT001: Function year needs a datetime, date or time as argument 1, not an arg of type IRI_ID (243) on the http://dbpedia.org/sparql endpoint (Virtuoso 7.20).
prefix dbo: <http://dbpedia.org/ontology/>
prefix dbp: <http://dbpedia.org/property/>

select ?p ?d (year(?d) as ?y){

   ?ap a dbo:Person; dbp:birthDate ?d

} limit 100

The dbp:birthDate predicate contains both xsd:date values and IRI references and guarding against non-temporal values is neither straightforward nor efficient.
Same behaviour for all temporal functions (SPARQL 1.1 Query Language § 17.4.5), which would be expected to raise an error, i.e. to return an unbound value, if the argument is not a temporal value.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE dbo_Person (id Int32, name String, birthDate DateTime, birthPlace String);
INSERT INTO dbo_Person (id, name, birthDate, birthPlace) VALUES (1, 'Alice', '1980-01-01 00:00:00', 'http://dbpedia.org/resource/Place1');
INSERT INTO dbo_Person (id, name, birthDate, birthPlace) VALUES (2, 'Bob', NULL, 'http://dbpedia.org/resource/Place2');
SELECT name, birthDate, toYear(birthDate) AS birthYear FROM dbo_Person WHERE birthDate IS NOT NULL;
SELECT name, birthPlace FROM dbo_Person WHERE birthPlace = 'http://dbpedia.org/resource/Place1' OR birthDate IS NULL;
key_bug_features: SPARQL functions fail with non-temporal argument types
